summary: Iteration 2 (Referral feature) testing — backend + frontend. Wrote 16 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_referral.py, all PASSED. UI flows validated end-to-end: client login (+40721000001), /client dashboard shows referral card, /client/recomanda shows large code + copy/whatsapp/share buttons + editable message + history, public /refer/{code} shows referrer name & 10% discount, form submits successfully → success screen, admin /admin/recomandari shows 5 KPI cards + rows with status selects, admin /admin/setari shows Program Recomandare card with discount + enabled + template-referral_share, customer detail /admin/clienti/{id} has Recomandări tab and header shows referral code (D2PGZP8Z). Ineligible client (+40721000004) correctly returns eligible=false.

ui_bugs.issue: Editable message preview only substitutes {link} and {discount} placeholders; {referrer_name} and {code} remain literal in the shared/copied text. This means WhatsApp share message contains raw '{code}' text unless template avoids these tokens.

action_items: Update /app/frontend/src/pages/client/Referrals.jsx message substitution to also replace {code} → info.code and {referrer_name} → customer name (available from client-auth/me or referrer). Alternatively, backend can substitute all placeholders before returning whatsapp_template.
Optional: also apply the same substitution when copying via btn-copy-message and btn-share-whatsapp so the recipient never sees literal {...} tokens.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py PATCH /api/referrals/{rid} accepts arbitrary dict body without a Pydantic model — consider a StatusUpdate schema to restrict allowed fields (currently caller could set any field on the referral doc).
server.py _ensure_referral_code uses a random collision retry then falls back to prefix+random digits — good, but the fallback code is 9 chars which breaks the '8-char' invariant advertised. Minor.
Public POST /refer/{code} does not rate-limit — a malicious actor could spam customer/lead creation. Consider IP or code-based throttling.
server.py refer_submit uses variable name 'ref' twice (line 806 for referrer lookup result & line 859 for Referral instance) — no bug but confusing shadowing.
Frontend Refer.jsx: no phone-format validation before submit; backend accepts any non-empty string.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@artjunkie.ro / ArtJunkie123!  |  Client eligible: +40721000001 (OTP via demo_code)  |  Client ineligible: +40721000004
seed_data_creation: None persistent — all TEST_ customers/leads created during tests are cleaned up. Referral rows have no DELETE endpoint, so ~2 TEST rows may remain in db.referrals (harmless, referrer_customer_id points to real client).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 2 referral endpoints verified. If main agent fixes the message-template placeholder substitution issue, re-run only /client/recomanda UI check to confirm {code} and {referrer_name} are properly substituted in the visible message preview.
rca of the issue: Message template placeholder substitution: In /app/frontend/src/pages/client/Referrals.jsx lines 21-23, only {link} and {discount} are chained via .replace(). The seed template stored in settings.templates.referral_share references {code} and {referrer_name} tokens as well, and those remain unsubstituted in the visible/copied message. Fix: add .replace('{code}', info.code || '').replace('{referrer_name}', customerName || '') where customerName comes from client-auth/me.
